title: Governance Engine Enhancement: Condition Verification Protocol
summary: Add a condition verification mode to the governance evaluation engine that independently validates whether MODIFY conditions have been satisfied before execution attestation is permitted. This addresses a governance gap identified during the execution of Decision #3 (Corpus Ratification v2), where the same entity that recommended the MODIFY conditions also declared them satisfied without independent validation.
body: During the execution of Decision #3 (re-evaluation of Proposal #002, Corpus Ratification), the Algorithmic Manager recommended MODIFY with four conditions: (1) fix corrupted corpus file, (2) spot-check Tier 3 files, (3) regenerate manifest hash, (4) add US Constitution within 30 days. All four conditions were satisfied — but the verification of satisfaction was performed by the same Claude instance that generated the conditions. The Human Executor signed the on-chain attestation based on this self-verification. This creates a governance gap: there is no independent verification step between condition generation and condition satisfaction claims. The smart contract records hashes, not content — it proves THAT a record was attested, not THAT the claims within it are true. This proposal adds two capabilities to the governance engine: (1) A --verify-conditions flag on evaluate.py that accepts an execution record JSON and re-evaluates whether the stated conditions are actually met by examining current repository state, file contents, and hashes. (2) A pre-attestation check that runs the verification before the Human Executor signs, producing a machine-readable pass/fail report. This is not a full re-evaluation (which would cost API credits). It is a targeted, automated check that can run locally without API calls for most condition types (file existence, hash verification, content validation).

governanceGap.description: No separation of concerns between condition generation and condition satisfaction verification. Self-verification by the same entity that generated the conditions.
governanceGap.precedent: Decision #3 — Corpus Ratification v2 MODIFY conditions verified by same Claude instance

proposedChanges.change: Add --verify-conditions flag that accepts an execution record JSON path and validates each stated condition against current repo state
Standalone verification script that checks: file existence, content hash matching, corpus manifest integrity, and other machine-verifiable conditions without requiring API calls
Add verification step before attestation in the CI pipeline

verificationTypes.description: Verify that a file's current keccak256 hash matches a claimed hash
Verify that a file exists at a claimed path
Verify that file content matches its metadata (e.g., paper title matches actual content)
verificationTypes.method: Check first N lines for title/author metadata consistency

verificationTypes.description: Verify all corpus files match manifest hashes and metadata headers match content
For conditions requiring judgment (e.g., 'adequate diversity'), use API call for independent re-assessment
verificationTypes.note: Only used when machine-verifiable checks are insufficient

costImpact: Minimal. Most verification types are local file operations with zero API cost. Semantic verification is the exception and should be used sparingly.
action: Implement verify.py, update evaluate.py with --verify-conditions flag, update CI workflow, and run retroactive verification on Decision #3 execution record as validation test.
